title: Why didnt Gendo age at all during the timeskip
display_title: Why didnt Gendo age at all during the timeskip
wikitext:
[]: Why didn't Gendo age at all during the timeskip?             Quote Post by Dima » Wed Apr 09, 2014 1:46 pm [Name corrected for grammar cuz why not. Was "Why Gendo didn't aged at all during timeskip?" - Monk] Rebuild of Evangelion 1.11 and 2.22 vs Rebuild of Evangelion 3.33 Okay i think it is pretty obvious that Gendo didn't aged at all during the timeskip. Except the pilots all the other characters like Misaro, Ritsuko, Fuyutsuki etc aged normally something that is very obvious. Gendo from the other hand seems like he is the same person even if the visor and the bears are covering half of his face. The question is why though. I think the visor explains it all. First of all Gendo is not blind. Second if this is Lorenz's visor i doubt Gendo took it by force or for a reason like ''to look more cool''. I think the visor means that Lorenz passed the ''knowledge'' or something similar to Gendo. As we know Seele has been around from always so they cannot be considered normal human beings. This wouldn't mean necessarily that all Seele members should be young but a) we never see them in NTE and b) they might revieved the ''knowledge'' after a certain age''. I don't know if my theory is correct but the visor must mean something and it may be connected with the fact that Gendo hasn't aged at all. Also i think there was a magazine cover that was showing Gendo with the visor off and holding it in his hands. I remember i saw the cover here so if anyone has a link....

[]:             Quote Post by Gob Hobblin » Wed Apr 09, 2014 1:50 pm Well, the kids didn't, either...and you can argue that none of the other Nerv personnel did. Exposure to LCL? Or N3I?

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Wed Apr 09, 2014 1:51 pm Not everybody ages fast, visually so- particularly if one is taking the correct steps to preserve one's appearance.

[]:             Quote Post by Dima » Wed Apr 09, 2014 2:00 pm Gendo before timeskip is 48 years old. This means in Q he is 62 so he should look a little different. For reference Fuyutsuki was 60 years old before timeskip.

[]:             Quote Post by Dataprime » Wed Apr 09, 2014 2:08 pm He feeds off of the Reis to keep up his youthful appearance

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Wed Apr 09, 2014 2:13 pm It's obvious, he bonded with the Key of Nebuchanezzar, and that kept him from aging. As for the visor, my guess is he was blinded by debris or the flash from an N2 mine or something during or after N3I, and had to have cyborg implants to augment his vision. Maybe Gendo is a composite character in 3.0? Meant to fill the roles of both his original character and Keele?

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Wed Apr 09, 2014 4:04 pm My guess is cybernetic implants, it would be a shame if his grand plan he prepared for 24 years now got to waste because he stupidly got an heart attack or something else related to age, so he didn't took any risks and fitted a truckload of implants to be sure that his body will stay in optimal shape when the time of the final phase of his plan arrives. Fuyutsuki visibly refused to take the same treatment, maybe because he doesn't want to lost what little humanity he has left (if only on the purely physical sense), or because he's so jaded now that he wouldn't really mind if he happened to die before the completion of the plan, this way he would escape from any possibility of confronting WILLE if they fail. The visor is just the "visible tip of the iceberg", it's like Khil in NGE : EoE revealed that he was so full of cybernetic implants that he was closer to a Terminator than a human being, all to prolong his life long enough to supervise the ritual for Instrumentality, and his visor was the only visible part of it. Maybe the visor has the double function of keeping his sight optimal (after all he already needed glasses before the time skip) and to serve as a direct interface to control neo-NERV automated installations. But I don't think that the visor was passed from Khil, since the monolith was Khil's body all along, and since millenia ago, so it's ore probable that it's simply a visor specifically created for him. Besides the visor has a very streamlined and sober look, which would perfectly fit something Gendo designed himself. As for the magazine cover with Gendo taking off the visor, IIRC it was from a doujin illustration, although in Q the visor doesn't cover Gendo's ears, so it's possible that he can take it off.

[]: Re: Why Gendo didn't aged at all during timeskip?             Quote Post by Kendrix » Thu Apr 10, 2014 3:11 am Dima wrote: Also i think there was a magazine cover that was showing Gendo with the visor off and holding it in his hands. I remember i saw the cover here so if anyone has a link....

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu Apr 10, 2014 11:24 am Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: @Kendrix: About that soul comment... assuming that we took it at face value and that Gendo has found some way to live without his soul (and therefore halting aging, etc, etc.) d'you think he could have put his soul into Evangelion 13?

[]:             Quote Post by Yaywalter » Thu Apr 10, 2014 3:53 pm Because he's Sean Connery.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Thu Apr 10, 2014 7:21 pm Think I'm gonna split out this tangent about Misato. EDIT: Done .

[]:             Quote Post by Dima » Fri Apr 11, 2014 5:20 am Why is this always happening with my threads? Someone will say something off-topic, the others will continue and mods will decide to split the discussion...

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Fri Apr 11, 2014 5:28 am I do a lot of splitting, and I've never noticed any patterns.

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Fri Apr 11, 2014 5:32 am ElMariachi wrote: In a soul gem of course, again it's all this fucking bunnycat's fault!

[]:             Quote Post by Dima » Fri Apr 11, 2014 5:39 am While cybernetic implants may be the reason i don't think we can be sure at this point. While we don't know Lorenz's age the dude looks way older then Gendo (NGE talking) and he has way more reasons to have cybernetic implants. Gendo from the other hand didn't had any health problems and saying he used cybernetic implants for looking younger is a bit too much IMO.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Apr 11, 2014 6:15 am ^ Take in account that Gendo had to live 14 years in a very spartan environment, unlike Kheel, so maybe he preemptively installed implants on him to prevent these living conditions to affect him physically. Fuyutsuki's comment that Shinji should see Gendo's living conditions to turn him to their side imply that they have really shitty living conditions, and indeed look at how completely ravaged Fuyutsuki is after 14 years in neo-NERV. Okay, the fact he's now 74 is probably a big reason, but take in account that in 3.0, Gendo is two years older than Fuyu before the time skip, yet he doesn't looks nearly as old as Fuyu during 1.0 and 2.0. Shinoyami65 wrote: Next he'll summon the power of AI YO and become Giant Naked Gendo (Damn HomuMado GenYui parallels).

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Fri Apr 11, 2014 6:55 am Dima wrote: While cybernetic implants may be the reason i don't think we can be sure at this point. While we don't know Lorenz's age the dude looks way older then Gendo (NGE talking) and he has way more reasons to have cybernetic implants. Gendo from the other hand didn't had any health problems and saying he used cybernetic implants for looking younger is a bit too much IMO.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Apr 11, 2014 7:10 am I was thinking, maybe the eventual implants was to give to Gendo a direct interface with all of neo-NERV installations, he seems to still spend most of his days in his old office, which is completely destroyed and doesn't seem to have a terminal in the desk, so he must receive information and transmit instructions to his installations somehow. A nice side-effect being that it delays his aging. The injuries during the events surrounding Third Impact are a great possibility too.

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Fri Apr 11, 2014 5:52 pm ElMariachi wrote: I was thinking, maybe the eventual implants was to give to Gendo a direct interface with all of neo-NERV installations, he seems to still spend most of his days in his old office, which is completely destroyed and doesn't seem to have a terminal in the desk, so he must receive information and transmit instructions to his installations somehow. A nice side-effect being that it delays his aging. The injuries during the events surrounding Third Impact are a great possibility too.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sun Apr 13, 2014 9:25 am Nah. Too wild a speculation. Lots of stuff in the geofront is probably automatic and has aways been. (Compare ep 11s "completely autonomous colony" talk. )
